channel.title: Photo Feeds
channel.description: Combined feed of recent photo essays from The Atlantic, The Guardian, The Boston Globe, NYT, and Reuters.

item.title: [The Guardian — Photos of the Day] 86 beams of light and an ICE Out protest: photos of the day – Wednesday
item.source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day
item.description:
Gaza City, Gaza — Mourners grieve after two Palestinians were killed in an Israeli airstrike on a tent sheltering displaced people in the Sheikh Ijlin area. More than 1,100 people in Gaza have been killed since a US-brokered October ceasefire between Hamas and Israel was agreed. — Photograph: Bilal Osama/APAImages/Shutterstock

Source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day

item.title: [The Guardian — Photos of the Day] 86 beams of light and an ICE Out protest: photos of the day – Wednesday
item.source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day
item.description:
Sloviansk, Ukraine — A woman and her two-year-old daughter are evacuated by the White Angels, a unit of the police specialised in evacuation and rescue, because the family’s home is in one of the districts of the city most heavily shelled by Russian forces. — Photograph: Maria Senovilla/EPA

Source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day

item.title: [The Guardian — Photos of the Day] 86 beams of light and an ICE Out protest: photos of the day – Wednesday
item.source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day
item.description:
Catia La Mar, Venezuela — Rescuers and volunteers work at a collapsed building after last month’s twin earthquakes, which killed more than 4,700 people. — Photograph: Federico Parra/AFP/Getty Images

Source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day

item.title: [The Guardian — Photos of the Day] 86 beams of light and an ICE Out protest: photos of the day – Wednesday
item.source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day
item.description:
New York, US — Protesters attend the ICE Out: Justice for Lorenzo and Johan demonstration in Foley Square, calling for the US Immigration and Customs Enforcement agency to be abolished after the fatal shootings of Lorenzo Salgado Araujo and Johan Sebastián Durán Guerrero. — Photograph: Sarah Yenesel/EPA

Source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day

item.title: [The Guardian — Photos of the Day] 86 beams of light and an ICE Out protest: photos of the day – Wednesday
item.source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day
item.description:
Nairobi, Kenya — A member of the Nyahbingh Order, one of the oldestRastafarian subgroups, smokes after the high court dismissed a petition by the Rastafari Society of Kenya calling for the legalisation of cannabis. — Photograph: Daniel Irungu/EPA

Source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day

item.title: [The Guardian — Photos of the Day] 86 beams of light and an ICE Out protest: photos of the day – Wednesday
item.source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day
item.description:
Johannesburg, South Africa — Malawian nationals carry their belonging as they line up to board a bus before repatriation after a wave of often violent anti-immigration protests and campaigns across the country. — Photograph: Marco Longari/AFP/Getty Images

Source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day

item.title: [The Guardian — Photos of the Day] 86 beams of light and an ICE Out protest: photos of the day – Wednesday
item.source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day
item.description:
Rongcheng, China — Fishers return to their boats at Ailun Bay Marine Ranch after Typhoon Bavi. The storm, one of the worst to hit China in a decade, caused deaths and widespread damage across the region. — Photograph: Yang Zhili/VCG/Getty Images

Source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day

London, UK — The British prime minister, Keir Starmer, leaves Downing Street to attend his final PMQs before he stands down as Labour party leader. — Photograph: Carl Court/Getty Images

item.title: [The Guardian — Photos of the Day] 86 beams of light and an ICE Out protest: photos of the day – Wednesday
item.source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day
item.description:
Rome, Italy — Greenpeace activists place ice statues depicting a farmer and a builder in front of the Colosseum during a protest to highlight the effects of global heating on vulnerable workers. — Photograph: Guglielmo Mangiapane/Reuters

Source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day

item.title: [The Guardian — Photos of the Day] 86 beams of light and an ICE Out protest: photos of the day – Wednesday
item.source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day
item.description:
Nice, France — People gather to mark the 10th anniversary of the Nice terror attack as 86 beams of light representing the 86 people killed fill the sky. More than 450 others were injured when a lorry was deliberately driven into crowds of people celebrating Bastille Day on the Promenade des Anglais in 2016. — Photograph: Valéry Hache/AFP/Getty Images

Source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day

item.title: [Reuters — The Wider Image] Sidelines Beyond Traditions Glimpses Wimbledons Fierce Sporting Soul
item.description:
Sofia Kenin of the U.S. in action during her second round match against Amanda Anisimova of the U.S. REUTERS/Toby Melville

Source: Reuters — The Wider Image

item.description:
Bangkok, Thailand — A family member receives the body of a victim at the police hospital morgue after a fatal fire at a city pub. — Photograph: Athit Perawongmetha/Reuters

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] Winners of the International Aerial Photographer of the Year
item.description:
Vitaly Golovatyuk / The 2nd International Aerial Photographer of the Year — Birdie in the Sky. First-place winner. Birdie is sitting on the water of a tiny, crystal-clear lake with reflection of the sky and surrounded by red grass, somewhere in Dongtai, Yancheng, Jiangsu province, China.

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] Winners of the International Aerial Photographer of the Year
item.description:
Kah-Wai Lin / The 2nd International Aerial Photographer of the Year — Thunder of Hooves. Third-place winner. At sunset in Cappadocia, Turkey, Yilki horses surge across the dusty plateau. Raised by villagers and released to roam free, they embody resilience and tradition, moving like a river of fire beneath the fading Anatolian sky.

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] Winners of the International Aerial Photographer of the Year
item.description:
Chris Gurton / The 2nd International Aerial Photographer of the Year — Potato Ridging. A Caterpillar Challenger ridges soil ready for planting potatoes while gulls circle, looking for worms in the freshly turned soil. Assington, Suffolk, U.K.

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] Winners of the International Aerial Photographer of the Year
item.description:
Koki Dote / The 2nd International Aerial Photographer of the Year — Eye. A large opening in the surface of the ice. Cracks radiating from it give the impression that something is watching from within.

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] Winners of the International Aerial Photographer of the Year
item.description:
Giacomo Feroldi / The 2nd International Aerial Photographer of the Year — Painted Fields. In spring, the fields of South Moravia in Czech Republic are filled with a variety of colors, mainly due to the sowing of rapeseed. The crops follow lines that look painted. A tree with a leafy canopy casts its shadow over the scene.

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] Winners of the International Aerial Photographer of the Year
item.description:
Kevin Nyun / The 2nd International Aerial Photographer of the Year — The Factory. Sunset light over Southern Utah’s iconic butte.

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] Winners of the International Aerial Photographer of the Year
item.description:
Koki Shinoda / The 2nd International Aerial Photographer of the Year — Commerson’s Dolphin and Giant Kelp. Commerson’s dolphins were swimming near giant kelp. The giant kelp makes the dolphins look very small. Taken in the Falkland Islands.

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] Winners of the International Aerial Photographer of the Year
item.description:
Sanghamitra Sarkar / The 2nd International Aerial Photographer of the Year — Wari. Second-place winner. A gathering of Warkaris during Maharashtra’s Wari pilgrimage forms a circular pattern around the central palkhi carrying sacred padukas. Dressed in white, devotees sit in rows, creating a powerful visual of unity, devotion, and harmony.

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] Winners of the International Aerial Photographer of the Year
item.description:
Peter O'Hara / The 2nd International Aerial Photographer of the Year — Cloud City Mount Yanapaccha. Mount Yanapaccha; 17,913 feet; Peru. Photographers stood on the summit early in the morning above a sea of clouds and sent the drone up into a magnificent sunrise. Surrounded by three dramatic peaks, it was one of those rare moments that felt almost surreal.

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] Winners of the International Aerial Photographer of the Year
item.description:
Cody Roberts / The 2nd International Aerial Photographer of the Year — Event Horizon. Like the Greek hero Icarus who soared too close to the sun, approaching the boundary beyond which nothing escapes, how close can you get? A USGS aircraft soars next to the historic eruption of Mauna Loa on Hawaii in 2022.

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] Winners of the International Aerial Photographer of the Year
item.description:
Fabio Pappalettera / The 2nd International Aerial Photographer of the Year — Tumbling White Horse. The photographer was riding in northeast Italy and catching some stills of a wonderful white horse, when suddenly he rolled over to scratch his back, raising the dust—and the epic moment was frozen forever.

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] Winners of the International Aerial Photographer of the Year
item.description:
Judith Kuhn / The 2nd International Aerial Photographer of the Year — Maloja. Fascinating from above: the serpentines of the Maloja Pass. Shortly before, it had snowed intensely for a few days and the larches had not yet completely shed their autumn dress.

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] Winners of the International Aerial Photographer of the Year
item.description:
Dylan De Haas / The 2nd International Aerial Photographer of the Year — Circle of Life. Ningaloo Reef, Western Australia. A tiger shark carries a green turtle in its jaws, revealing a rarely witnessed moment of predation. Predator and prey meet at the surface, exposing the raw tension and ecological reality that sustains ocean balance.

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] Winners of the International Aerial Photographer of the Year
item.description:
Abhijeet Sawant / The 2nd International Aerial Photographer of the Year — Embrace of the Sky. In this aerial panorama of Iceland, a gentle rainbow arcs across the sky, casting soft hues over dark slopes and deep-blue waters. A lone road winds toward distant shores in perfect, breathless harmony.

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] Winners of the International Aerial Photographer of the Year
item.description:
Yau Tat Hui / The 2nd International Aerial Photographer of the Year — Night of the Century. A tragic photo; more than 100 residents died.

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] Winners of the International Aerial Photographer of the Year
item.description:
Jiri Kostal / The 2nd International Aerial Photographer of the Year — Dead Man Walking. Self-portrait on a frozen pond

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] Winners of the International Aerial Photographer of the Year
item.description:
Laszlo Siman / The 2nd International Aerial Photographer of the Year — Vineyard. A picture taken at dawn in spring in a vineyard in the Czech Republic. The cold mist colored the surface of the earth, and in some places the ground was still frozen.

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] Winners of the International Aerial Photographer of the Year
item.description:
Zhengze Xu / The 2nd International Aerial Photographer of the Year — Oryx Walking on Dune. Taken from a helicopter in the Namib Desert, Namibia. It was late afternoon and sunset was beautiful, illuminating dunes with golden color and amazing shadow lines. This single oryx was walking on the dunes with its shadow and footprints.

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] Winners of the International Aerial Photographer of the Year
item.description:
Cédric Tamani / The 2nd International Aerial Photographer of the Year — Magadi Dream. Picture taken from a helicopter, Lake Magadi, Kenya

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] Winners of the International Aerial Photographer of the Year
item.description:
Thibault Gerbaldi / The 2nd International Aerial Photographer of the Year — Aarti Under the Stars. Each night in Varanasi, India, several thousand people gather to attend the Ganga Aarti, an ancient ceremony. Population pressures lead many devotees to follow the prayer from the Ganges itself aboard tightly packed boats.

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] Winners of the International Aerial Photographer of the Year
item.description:
Alexandra Moldovan / The 2nd International Aerial Photographer of the Year — Basking in Divine Light. Views on a moody morning over the treetops of Denmark. This light and atmosphere allowed the photographer to create a graphical, geometrically pleasing image with the sunbeams pushing through the treetops.

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] Winners of the International Aerial Photographer of the Year
item.description:
Dylan De Haas / The 2nd International Aerial Photographer of the Year — Electric Blue. Rockingham, Western Australia. Two Indo-Pacific bottlenose dolphins glide through impossibly clear blue water, their bodies suspended above shifting sand patterns. The vivid tones and flowing textures turn a quiet moment into something almost surreal.

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] Winners of the International Aerial Photographer of the Year
item.description:
Michiko Kimura / The 2nd International Aerial Photographer of the Year — Arctic Pulse Sámi Reindeer Migration. From above, a migration becomes a living heartbeat with more than 2,000 reindeer flowing northward across Finnmark, Norway. Guided by Sámi herders and ancient instinct, this seasonal movement reflects a rhythm older than borders, where survival and culture move as one.

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] Winners of the International Aerial Photographer of the Year
item.description:
Chengming Liu / The 2nd International Aerial Photographer of the Year — Emerald Waves at Dusk. Captured above the rolling hills of San Jose, California, at golden hour, this image reveals the Diablo Range at its greenest after winter rains, highlighting smooth contours and flowing patterns.

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.title: [The Guardian — Photos of the Day] The week around the world in 20 pictures
item.source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day
item.description:
London, England — Philippines’ Alexandra Eala returns to Poland’s Iga Swiatek during their third round match at Wimbledon. — Photograph: Henry Nicholls/AFP/Getty

Source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day

item.title: [The Guardian — Photos of the Day] The week around the world in 20 pictures
item.source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day
item.description:
Paris, France — A model presents a creation by the designer Manish Malhotra as part of his haute couture fall/winter 2026-27 at Paris fashion week. — Photograph: Sarah Meyssonnier/Reuters

Source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day

item.title: [The Guardian — Photos of the Day] The week around the world in 20 pictures
item.source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day
item.description:
Paris, France — Coralie holds her pet parrot, Dali, up to a mist fountain to cool him down on the banks of the Seine. — Photograph: Tom Nicholson/Reuters

Source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day

item.title: [The Guardian — Photos of the Day] The week around the world in 20 pictures
item.source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day
item.description:
Calonge, Spain — A person points towards a wildfire near Calonge. Barcelona set a new heat record this week with temperatures of 40.5C, Spanish meteorologists said, while in France a 22-year-old firefighter died after tackling a blaze in the Alps, the French interior ministry reported. — Photograph: Bruna Casas/Reuters

Source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day

item.title: [The Guardian — Photos of the Day] The week around the world in 20 pictures
item.source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day
item.description:
Cercosa, Portugal — Firefighters battle the flames of the Vouzela wildfire. Raging infernos have laid waste to large areas of southern Europe in recent days, prompting the EU to scramble firefighters and water-bearing planes to help national services overwhelmed by simultaneous blazes. — Photograph: Paulo Cunha/EPA

Source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day

item.title: [The Guardian — Photos of the Day] The week around the world in 20 pictures
item.source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day
item.description:
Havana, Cuba — A street vendor uses a portable lamp to sell goods along the Malecón during a nationwide blackout. Cuba was already struggling to keep the lights on before Donald Trump imposed an oil blockade in January, which has depleted the already dwindling supply of fuel for Cuba’s power plants. — Photograph: Adalberto Roque/AFP/Getty

Source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day

item.title: [The Guardian — Photos of the Day] The week around the world in 20 pictures
item.source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day
item.description:
Washington DC, US — Disappointed fans watch the World Cup match between USA and Belgium at the National Mall. The buildup to the match had been tinged with controversy after Fifa suspended the red card the US striker Folarin Balogun was given in the last 32 against Bosnia and Herzegovina. — Photograph: Eric Lee/Reuters

Source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day

item.title: [The Guardian — Photos of the Day] The week around the world in 20 pictures
item.source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day
item.description:
Washington DC, US — Fireworks explode during the Great American State Fair on the National Mall. Donald Trump hailed the ‘unmatched achievement and unlimited potential’ of the US in a triumphalist address marking the country’s 250th anniversary. — Photograph: Rod Lamkey/AP

Source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day

item.title: [The Guardian — Photos of the Day] The week around the world in 20 pictures
item.source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day
item.description:
Washington DC, US — Children sit on the floor as Donald Trump speaks on the first day of trading for Trump Accounts in the Oval Office. — Photograph: Shawn Thew/UPI/Shutterstock

Source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day

item.title: [The Guardian — Photos of the Day] The week around the world in 20 pictures
item.source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day
item.description:
Washington DC, US — A commuter sits as members of the group Patriot Front ride the metro on the 250th anniversary of US independence on the Fourth of July. The neo-fascist hate group has gained visibility as those connected to white supremacy organisations have been widely embraced by the Trump administration. — Photograph: Cheney Orr/Reuters

Source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day

item.title: [The Guardian — Photos of the Day] The week around the world in 20 pictures
item.source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day
item.description:
La Guaira, Venezuela — Nuns gather after accompanying a burial for earthquake victims in the aftermath of the 24 June earthquakes at La Esperanza cemetery. Many Venezuelans have expressed anger at what they see as the US-backed government’s inadequate response to the disaster before international teams arrived. — Photograph: Adriano Machado/Reuters

Source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day

item.title: [The Guardian — Photos of the Day] The week around the world in 20 pictures
item.source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day
item.description:
La Guaira, Venezuela — People transport belongings inside a damaged building in the aftermath of the 24 June earthquakes. Venezuela’s interim president, Delcy Rodríguez, defended her country’s emergency response to the twin earthquakes that have killed more than 3,000 people, vowing Venezuela would not descend into social unrest. — Photograph: Adriano Machado/Reuters

Source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day

item.title: [The Guardian — Photos of the Day] The week around the world in 20 pictures
item.source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day
item.description:
Kyiv, Ukraine — A resident waits for rescuers who are searching for her relative inside an apartment building hit during Russian missile and drone strikes. Volodymyr Zelenskyy has argued for Ukraine to be allowed to join Nato at its annual summit, saying it would be wrong to exclude a country that had built strong defences in its long struggle against the Russian invasion. — Photograph: Valentyn Ogirenko/Reuters

Source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day

item.title: [The Guardian — Photos of the Day] The week around the world in 20 pictures
item.source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day
item.description:
Kyiv, Ukraine — A firefighter works at the site of a Russian missile strike. Donald Trump told Volodymyr Zelenskyy at the Nato summit that Ukraine may be allowed to manufacture Patriot missile interceptors to counter Russian ballistic attacks. — Photograph: Gleb Garanich/Reuters

Source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day

item.title: [The Guardian — Photos of the Day] The week around the world in 20 pictures
item.source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day
item.description:
Kyiv, Ukraine — A woman holds her daughter after a Russian missile strike on a residential building. A wave of Russian missiles and drones struck across Ukraine this week, killing 21 people and heavily damaging apartment blocks and other buildings in an attack on the eve of a Nato summit in Turkey that has exposed widening gaps in Ukraine’s air defences. — Photograph: Genya Savilov/AFP/Getty

Source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day

item.title: [The Guardian — Photos of the Day] The week around the world in 20 pictures
item.source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day
item.description:
Najaf, Iraq — Mourners carry the coffin of Iran’s slain supreme leader Ali Khamenei at the Imam Ali shrine. The scale of the six-day, five-city funeral in Iran and Iraq was conceived to relay political and religious messages of resistance to the rest of the world. — Photograph: Hussein Faleh/AFP/Getty

Source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day

item.title: [The Guardian — Photos of the Day] The week around the world in 20 pictures
item.source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day
item.description:
Gaza City — A girl fills containers with water drawn from the leftovers of underground pipes at a camp for people displaced by war. Israel identified about 1,000 potential targets a day during the first two years of the wars in Gaza and Lebanon with its command and control system, according to a presentation by the country’s largest arms supplier, Elbit Systems. — Photograph: Omar Al-Qattaa/AFP/Getty

Source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day

item.title: [The Guardian — Photos of the Day] The week around the world in 20 pictures
item.source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day
item.description:
Gaza City — Palestinian football fans gather to watch the World Cup match between Argentina and Egypt. Mohamed al-Wahidi, a Palestinian aid worker who had organised screenings of World Cup matches in Gaza, was killed by an Israeli missile strike just before the match on Tuesday evening. — Photograph: Mohammed Skaik/TheNews2/Cover Images

Source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day

item.title: [The Guardian — Photos of the Day] A Tour de France picnic and Typhoon Bavi: photos of the day – Friday
item.source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day
item.description:
London, UK — A worker pushes a specially designed crate carrying the Bayeux tapestry into the British Museum. The arrival of the 11th-century artwork depicting the Norman conquest marked the first time it has returned to England in nearly 1,000 years. — Photograph: Richard A Brooks/AFP/Getty Images

Source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day

item.title: [The Guardian — Photos of the Day] A Tour de France picnic and Typhoon Bavi: photos of the day – Friday
item.source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day
item.description:
Hagetmau, France — The peloton passes spectators during the start of stage 7 of the Tour de France between Hagetmau and Bordeaux. Tadej Pogacar leads the standings board after his victory in stage 6 of the race. — Photograph: Anne-Christine Poujoulat/AFP/Getty Images

Source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day

item.title: [The Guardian — Photos of the Day] A Tour de France picnic and Typhoon Bavi: photos of the day – Friday
item.source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day
item.description:
Vulcano, Italy — People immerse themselves in the natural thermal mud pool of the island off Sicily. — Photograph: Marco Bertorello/AFP/Getty Images

Source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day

item.title: [The Guardian — Photos of the Day] A Tour de France picnic and Typhoon Bavi: photos of the day – Friday
item.source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day
item.description:
Konya, Turkey — An aerial view of the Selçuklu flower garden, where approximately 385,000 flowers of 84 varieties from 28 different species bloom across 11,500 sq metres. The garden attracts thousands of visitors each year. — Photograph: Serhat Cetinkaya/Getty Images

Source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day

item.title: [The Guardian — Photos of the Day] A Tour de France picnic and Typhoon Bavi: photos of the day – Friday
item.source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day
item.description:
Zoige, China — Tourists visit Flower Lake during the peak summer travel season in Sichuan province. Flower Lake is an important water conservation area in the upper reaches of the Yellow River. — Photograph: VCG/Getty Images

Source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day

item.title: [The Guardian — Photos of the Day] A Tour de France picnic and Typhoon Bavi: photos of the day – Friday
item.source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day
item.description:
La Guaira, Venezuela — People displaced by last month’s double earthquake gather at a temporary shelter. The official death toll from the twin quakes passed ​3,810 this week as Venezuela’s interim president, Delcy Rodriguez, renewed calls ​for international sanctions on the country to be lifted to assist the recovery. — Photograph: Anadolu/Getty Images

Source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day

item.title: [The Guardian — Photos of the Day] A Tour de France picnic and Typhoon Bavi: photos of the day – Friday
item.source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day
item.description:
El Dabaa, Egypt — Fans gather to welcome the Egypt men’s football team at El Alamein airport before an official reception hosted by President Abdel Fatah al-Sisi. Egypt were knocked out of the 2026 World Cup by Argentina on Tuesday. — Photograph: Mohamed Abdel Ghany/Reuters

Source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day

item.title: [The Guardian — Photos of the Day] A Tour de France picnic and Typhoon Bavi: photos of the day – Friday
item.source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day
item.description:
Chichester, UK — A Bugatti (L) and an Alfa Romeo P3 (Tipo B) are driven up the hill during the Goodwood Festival of Speed, which runs until Sunday. — Photograph: Simon Galloway/Getty Images

Source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day

item.title: [The Guardian — Photos of the Day] A Tour de France picnic and Typhoon Bavi: photos of the day – Friday
item.source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day
item.description:
Henley, UK — Visitors watch fireworks as the sun sets over the Thames during the Henley festival. The annual music and arts festival relaxed its black tie dress code for audience members on its opening day because of the heatwave. — Photograph: James Robinson

Source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day

item.title: [The Guardian — Photos of the Day] A Tour de France picnic and Typhoon Bavi: photos of the day – Friday
item.source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day
item.description:
Dunsden Green, UK — The sun rises over countryside in Oxfordshire at the beginning of another hot day. Temperatures of 35C or higher have now been recorded in May, June and July of the same year for the first time in the UK, the Met Office has said. — Photograph: Geoffrey Swaine/Shutterstock

Source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day

item.title: [The Guardian — Photos of the Day] A Tour de France picnic and Typhoon Bavi: photos of the day – Friday
item.source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day
item.description:
Maidenhead, UK — A rower sculls on the Thames during the country’s third heatwave of 2026. The heatwave is to continue next week as forecasters confirm that last month was western Europe’s hottest June on record. — Photograph: Jonathan Brady/PA

Source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day

item.title: [The Guardian — Photos of the Day] A Tour de France picnic and Typhoon Bavi: photos of the day – Friday
item.source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day
item.description:
Die, France — A wildfire burns in the hills above the south-east French town. Wildfires have burned through twice as much land in France so far this year as in the same period in 2025, according to officials. France is facing its third heatwave since May. — Photograph: Manon Cruz/Reuters

Source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day

item.title: [The Guardian — Photos of the Day] A Tour de France picnic and Typhoon Bavi: photos of the day – Friday
item.source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day
item.description:
Almería, Spain — A firefighting helicopter dumps water over a wildfire in Los Gallardos. At least 12 people have been killed and 23 are unaccounted for after one of Spain’s deadliest wildfires broke out in the south-eastern province. — Photograph: Reuters

Source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day

item.title: [The Guardian — Photos of the Day] A Tour de France picnic and Typhoon Bavi: photos of the day – Friday
item.source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day
item.description:
Beijing, China — Two women carrying an umbrella walk in heavy rain in the Chinese capital. The city has issued its first amber rainstorm alert of the year and has evacuated nearly 100,000 residents. — Photograph: Maxim Shemetov/Reuters

Source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day

item.title: [The Guardian — Photos of the Day] A Tour de France picnic and Typhoon Bavi: photos of the day – Friday
item.source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day
item.description:
Keelung, Taiwan — Statues outside the Dianji temple are covered with protective tarpaulins before the arrival of Typhoon Bavi, which has already triggered a deadly landslide in the Philippines. — Photograph: I-Hwa Cheng/AFP/Getty Images

Source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day

item.title: [The Guardian — Photos of the Day] A Tour de France picnic and Typhoon Bavi: photos of the day – Friday
item.source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day
item.description:
Yilan, Taiwan — A fisher handles a rope near a fishing boat as waves crash over a breakwater before the arrival of Typhoon Bavi, which is forecast to be one of the strongest storms in decades. — Photograph: I-Hwa Cheng/AFP/Getty Images

Source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] Photos of the Week: Rabbit Beach, Puffin Census, Wild Horses
item.description:
Jens Büttner / DPA / Getty — A dancer welcomes visitors to the airfield grounds at the Airbeat One electronic-music festival in Neustadt-Glewe, Germany, on July 9, 2026. About 200,000 electronic-music fans from more than 60 countries were expected to attend the festival.

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] Photos of the Week: Rabbit Beach, Puffin Census, Wild Horses
item.description:
Samuel de Roman / Anadolu / Getty — An aloitador holds a wild horse during the first curro in Sabucedo, Spain, on July 4, 2026. The Rapa das Bestas of Sabucedo is a festival held every year during the first week of July, where aloitadors immobilize and cut the manes and tails of wild horses.

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] Photos of the Week: Rabbit Beach, Puffin Census, Wild Horses
item.description:
Jens Büttner / DPA / Getty — A fish eagle, about five weeks old, is lifted out of its nest by a utility worker for banding in Neustadt-Glewe, Germany, on July 6, 2026. Bird experts are currently banding the chicks that hatched a few weeks ago, with support from the energy provider WeMag.

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] Photos of the Week: Rabbit Beach, Puffin Census, Wild Horses
item.description:
Temilade Adelaja / Reuters — An Atlantic puffin, seen during the 2026 annual census, monitoring the seabirds’ breeding population, on the Inner Farne Islands, Northumberland, England, on July 6, 2026

Source: The Atlantic — Photography
